title: silene littorea subsp. adscendens
intro: Silene littorea subsp. adscendens, also known as sea campion, is a perennial plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is native to the coastal regions of Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of wounds, burns, and skin infections. The herbal preparations of Silene littorea subsp. adscendens include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which are used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin irritations.
